name: Cheesecake Hints
recipeInstructions:
1. Wrapping the pan with foil keeps it watertight.
2. If you grease the sides of the pan first, the surface will not crack when the cake starts to shrink on cooling (it always shrinks a little).
3. Chill thoroughly before unmolding. In fact, make cheesecakes one day ahead and leave in springform until ready to use.
4. The water bath pan must be at or below the level of the springform pan; do not exceed 1 inch (≥ in) of water.
5. Verify oven temperature accuracy.
6. After baking, turn off the oven. Do not open the door; let it sit for one hour. Then remove, place on a rack, and allow to cool to room temperature (about one hour).
7. Use 1 tablespoon cornstarch per 2 (8 oz) packages of cream cheese. Use Philadelphia brand cream cheese.
8. IMPORTANT: Use egg size specified in the recipe; if none is given, use large eggs.
